volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 3.2.880 - Section 4.1.953c)

sections.label: Section 3.2.880
sections.original: Quin tuós inimicos potius quám amicos vocas ?
sections.translation: But why don ’ t you invite your enemies to it rather than your friends ? For this day will I present to the guests a banquet so savoury , and I ’ ll season it with such a dulcet sweetness , that whoever shall taste each thing that ’ s seasoned , I ’ ll make that same person to gnaw off the ends of his own fingers .

sections.label: Section 3.2.885
sections.original: Quaeso hercle , prius quam quoiquam convivae dabis ,
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , before you shall present aught to the guests do you yourself first taste , and give some to your understrappers , that you may gnaw off the ends of your own pilfering hands .

sections.label: Section 3.2.888
sections.original: Fortasse haec tu nunc mihi non credis quae loquor .
sections.translation: Perhaps then you don ’ t believe me in the things that I say .

sections.label: Section 3.2.889
sections.original: Molestus ne sis , nimium iam tinnis ; non taces ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be troublesome ; you din me too much ; you don ’ t please me by it .

sections.label: Section 3.2.890
sections.original: em illíc ego habito . íntro abi et cenam coque .
sections.translation: See , there I live . ( Points to his house .) Do you go in-doors and cook the dinner , with all speed .

sections.label: Section 3.2.891b
sections.original: Quin tu is accubitum , et convivas cedo ,
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go , and take your place ? Go and find the guests ; the dinner ’ s spoiling already .

sections.label: Section 3.2.892b
sections.translation: Now , just look , please , at that young offshoot ; for he , too , is a good-for-nothing deputy-scullion for the cook . Truly I don ’ t know what now first to be on my guard against ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.895
sections.original: ita in aedibus sunt fures , praedo in proxumo est .
sections.translation: such thieves there are in my house , and there ’ s a robber close at hand . For my neighbour here , the father of Calidorus , a short time since , in the market-place , asked me by all means to be on my guard against his servant Pseudolus , not to put any trust in him ; for that he is on the hunt this day ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.900
sections.original: ut me , si posset , muliere intervorteret ;
sections.translation: if possible to dupe me out of the woman . He said that he had stoutly promised to him that he would get away Phœnicium from me by stratagem . I ’ ll now go indoors and give notice to my household , that no one must put any trust whatever in this Pseudolus .

sections.label: Section 4.1.905
sections.original: Si umquám quemquam di immórtales voluére esse auxilio ádiutum ,
sections.translation: If the immortal Gods ever did determine that any person should be assisted by their aid , now do they intend that Calidorus shall be preserved for me , and the procurer destroyed , inasmuch as they produced you for my assistant , so clever and so knowing a fellow . ( Looking back .) But where is he ? am I not a silly fellow to be thus talking to myself alone ? I ’ faith , he has put a trick upon myself , as I fancy ; myself one knave , I have been poorly on my guard against another knave .

sections.label: Section 4.1.910
sections.original: tum pól ego interii , homo si ílle abiit , neque hoc ópus quod volui hodie éfficiam .
sections.translation: By my troth I ’ m undone , if this fellow ’ s off , and I shall not carry into effect this day what I intended . But see , there he is , a statue that deserves a whipping ; how stately he does stalk along !

sections.label: Section 4.1.912
sections.original: ehem , te hércle ego circumspéctabam , nimis métuebam male , ne ábiisses .
sections.translation: How now ! By my faith I was looking about for you ; I was very greatly afraid that you were off .

sections.label: Section 4.1.913
sections.original: Fuit meum ófficium ut facerém , fateor .
sections.translation: It was my character to do so , I confess .

sections.label: Section 4.1.913a
sections.translation: Where were you loitering ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.913ab
sections.translation: Where I pleased .

sections.label: Section 4.1.914
sections.translation: That I know well enough already .

sections.label: Section 4.1.914b
sections.original: Quor érgo quod scis mé rogas ?
sections.translation: Why then do you ask me what you know ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.915
sections.translation: Why this I want , to put you in mind .

sections.label: Section 4.1.915b
sections.translation: Needing to be put in mind yourself , don ’ t you be putting me in mind .

sections.label: Section 4.1.916
sections.original: Nimis tándem ego aps te contémnor .
sections.translation: Really I am treated by you quite with contempt .

sections.label: Section 4.1.917
sections.translation: And why shouldn ’ t I treat you with contempt , I who have the repute of being a military gentleman ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.919
sections.original: Iam hoc vólo quod occeptúmst agi .
sections.translation: I want this then , which has been commenced , to be completed .

sections.label: Section 4.1.919b
sections.translation: Do you see me a-doing anything else ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.920
sections.translation: Therefore walk on briskly .

sections.label: Section 4.1.920b
sections.translation: No , I choose to go slowly .

sections.label: Section 4.1.921
sections.original: Haéc ea occásiost : dum ílle dormít , volo
sections.translation: This is the opportunity ; while this Harpax is asleep , I want you to be the first to accost him .

sections.label: Section 4.1.923
sections.translation: Why are you hurrying ? Softly ; don ’ t you fear . I wish Jupiter would so make it , that he were openly in the same place with me , whoever he is , that has arrived from the Captain .

sections.label: Section 4.1.925
sections.translation: Never a jot , by my troth , should he be a bit the better Harpax than I . Have good courage , I ’ ll have this business nicely accounted for to you . So by my tricks and lies would I put this military stranger in a fright that he himself would deny that he is the person that he is ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.930
sections.original: méque ut esse aútumet qui ípsus est .
sections.translation: and would believe me to be the person that he himself is .

sections.label: Section 4.1.930b
sections.translation: How can that be ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.931
sections.translation: You are murdering me when you ask me that .

sections.label: Section 4.1.931b
sections.translation: A clever fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.1.932
sections.original: Té quoque etiám dolis átque mendáciis ,
sections.translation: And so are you too , who are quite my equal with your mischievous tricks and lies ⋯

sections.label: Section 4.1.934
sections.translation: May Jupiter preserve you for me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.934b
sections.translation: Aye , and for myself .

sections.label: Section 4.1.935
sections.original: séd vide , ornátus hic me satin cóndecet ?
sections.translation: But look , does this dress become me quite well ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.935a
sections.translation: It suits very well .

sections.label: Section 4.1.935ab
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 4.1.936
sections.original: Tantúm tibi boni di immórtales duint quántum tu tibi óptes ;
sections.translation: May the Deities grant you as many blessings as you may wish for yourself . For if I were to wish for as many as you are deserving of , they would be less than nothing ; ( aside ) nor have I ever seen any one more of a rogue than this fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.1.938a
sections.translation: Do you say that to me ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.938ab
sections.translation: I ’ m silent . But what good turn shall I do you if you carry through this matter with management ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.940
sections.original: Potin út taceas ? memorem ímmemorem facit quí monet quod memor méminit .
sections.translation: Can ’ t you hold your tongue ? He that puts a man in mind of that which , remembering it , he does keep in mind , causes him to forget it . I recollect everything ; they are stored up in my breast ; my plans are cleverly laid .

sections.label: Section 4.1.942
sections.translation: This man ’ s an honest fellow .

sections.label: Section 4.1.942b
sections.translation: It is neither this person , then ( pointing to PSEUDOLUS ) , nor myself .

sections.label: Section 4.1.942c
sections.translation: But take care that you don ’ t be tripping .

sections.label: Section 4.1.943
sections.translation: So may the Gods love me —

sections.label: Section 4.1.943b
sections.original: Ita nón facient : mera iám mendacia fúndes .
sections.translation: They won ’ t do so ; you ’ ll be uttering sheer falsehoods then .

sections.label: Section 4.1.944
sections.original: Vt ego ób tuam , Simia , pérfidiam te amo et métuo et magni fácio .
sections.translation: How I do love you , Simmia , for your roguery , and both fear and laud you .

sections.label: Section 4.1.945
sections.original: Ego istúc aliis dare cóndidici : mi optrúdere non potes pálpum .
sections.translation: That I have learned to make a present of to others ; you can ’ t put your flatteries on me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.946
sections.original: Vt ego áccipiam te hodié lepide , ubi efféceris hoc opus ,
sections.translation: In how delightful a manner I shall receive you this day , when you have completed this matter .

sections.label: Section 4.1.946b
sections.translation: Ha , ha , ha !

sections.label: Section 4.1.947
sections.original: Lepidó victu , vino , únguentis et intér pocula pulpámentis ;
sections.translation: With nice viands , wine , perfumes , and titbits between our cups . There , too , shall be a charming damsel , who shall give you kiss upon kiss .

sections.label: Section 4.1.949
sections.translation: You will be receiving me in a delightful manner .

sections.label: Section 4.1.949b
sections.original: Immo si éfficies , tum fáxo magis id dícas .
sections.translation: Aye , and if you effect it , then I ’ ll make you say so still more .

sections.label: Section 4.1.950
sections.original: Nisi effécero , cruciabíliter cárnifex me accipito .
sections.translation: If I don ’ t effect it , do you , the executioner , take me off to torture . But make haste and point out to me where is the door of the procurer ’ s house .

sections.label: Section 4.1.952
sections.translation: ’ Tis the third hence .

sections.label: Section 4.1.952b
sections.translation: Hist ! hush ! the door ’ s opening .

sections.label: Section 4.1.952c
sections.translation: In my mind , I believe that the house is poorly .

sections.label: Section 4.1.953b
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.953c
sections.translation: Because , i ’ faith , it is vomiting forth the procurer .
